title: Immunoprecipitation, Decross-linking, and DNA Extraction
author: Vasso Makrantoni
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bn3xmgpn

steps.title: Prewash ( n × 15) μl...
steps.instruction: Prewash ( n × 15) μl of Protein G Dynabeads ( n = number of IP samples) in 1 mL, ice-cold 1× FA buffer containing 0.1% SDS, 1 millimolar (mM), PMSF and protease inhibitors with rotation for 00:05:00 . Place the microcentrifuge tubes on a magnet and discard the supernatant. (1/3)

steps.title: Prewash ( n × 15) μl...
steps.instruction: Prewash ( n × 15) μl of Protein G Dynabeads ( n = number of IP samples) in 1 mL, ice-cold 1× FA buffer containing 0.1% SDS, 1 millimolar (mM), PMSF and protease inhibitors with rotation for 00:05:00 . Place the microcentrifuge tubes on a magnet and discard the supernatant. (2/3)

steps.title: Prewash ( n × 15) μl...
steps.instruction: Prewash ( n × 15) μl of Protein G Dynabeads ( n = number of IP samples) in 1 mL, ice-cold 1× FA buffer containing 0.1% SDS, 1 millimolar (mM), PMSF and protease inhibitors with rotation for 00:05:00 . Place the microcentrifuge tubes on a magnet and discard the supernatant. (3/3)

steps.title: Add the antibody against the protein...
steps.instruction: Add the antibody against the protein of interest to 1 mL, chromatin extract (step 13 in protocol "Cell Lysis and Sonication") and 15 µL, prewashed Dynabeads and mix. The amount of SDS in immunoprecipitation can interfere with antibody binding efficiency therefore, lower amount of SDS can also be used. Either omit SDS in 2xFA buffer or reduce SDS added afterward to a final concentration of 0.05% before chromatin immunoprecipitation. The amount of antibody added should be in excess of the protein being immunoprecipitated and should be determined empirically. We use the following amounts of antibodies per IP: 10 µL, 9E10 (Tonbo Biosciences) 7.5 µL, 12CA5 (Roche) 10 µL, SV5-PK1 (Bio-Rad) 10 µL, anti-GFP (Roche) 5 µL, M2 FLAG (Sigma) No blocking and preclearing is required for the magnetic beads.

steps.title: Incubate on a rotating wheel at 4 °C Overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate on a rotating wheel at 4 °C Overnight .

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnet and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnet and discard the supernatant. Perform the following washes, using 1 ml per sample of Wash buffer on a rotating wheel for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatants after each wash.

steps.title: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash...
steps.instruction: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash buffer 1 - low salt on a rotating wheel for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Discard supernatant after wash.

steps.title: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash...
steps.instruction: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash buffer 2 - high salt on a rotating wheel for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Discard supernatant after wash.

steps.title: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash...
steps.instruction: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash buffer 3 on a rotating wheel for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Discard supernatant after wash.

steps.title: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash...
steps.instruction: Wash using 1 mL, ChIP Wash buffer 4 on a rotating wheel for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Discard supernatant after wash.

steps.title: Following the final wash, place the...
steps.instruction: Following the final wash, place the samples on magnetic rack and discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Move forward with
steps.instruction: Move forward with: reversal of cross-linking and isolation of DNA for qPCR, OR ChIP-seq preparation after the final wash

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2 mL, 10% slurry (wt/vol) in sterile water Chelex-100 resin directly to the washed Dynabeads (IP sample) and to 10 µL, thawed “Input” samples (see step 14 from protocol "Cell Lysis and Sonication"). Keep the Chelex beads in suspension while pipetting.

steps.title: Briefly vortex samples to mix the...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex samples to mix the slurry and boil for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Cool the tubes to Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Cool the tubes to Room temperature and quickly centrifuge condensate to the bottom of the tube at 400 x g, °C, . Proceed immediately to the next step. Variation in the amount of beads added can affect the specific signal–background ratio. Make sure to keep the slurry suspended while distributing.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 µL, Proteinase K (10 mg/ml) to each sample and incubate on a heat block at 55 °C for 00:30:00 with occasional mixing to resuspend beads.

steps.title: Boil the samples at 100 °C...
steps.instruction: Boil the samples at 100 °C for 00:10:00 to inactivate the Proteinase K.

steps.title: Spin the tubes briefly at 400...
steps.instruction: Spin the tubes briefly at 400 x g, °C, and carefully transfer approximately 120 µL, DNA supernatant , termed the IP sample in a new tube. Make sure not to transfer any Chelex resin as it can lead to loss of PCR signal.

steps.title: Store samples at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store samples at -20 °C . (PAUSE POINT).

steps.title: Purified DNA from step 12 can...
steps.instruction: Purified DNA from step 12 can be used in qPCR, following the subsequent steps. We use Luna Universal Probe qPCR Master Mix in a 10 μl reaction in a 96-well plate and run the following program:

steps.title: Set up the following reaction mix
steps.instruction: Set up the following reaction mix: 3 µL, DNA Template (for Input use 1:300, for IP use 1:6 dilutions) 0.125 µL, primer pair (20 μM each) 5 µL, master mix ddH 2 O up to 10 μl final volume

steps.title: Run the following program
steps.instruction: Run the following program: %s %s

steps.title: Perform data analysis using Microsoft Excel...
steps.instruction: Perform data analysis using Microsoft Excel software using the following formulas: Determine the ChIP enrichment (i.e., ChIP/Input value), by calculating ΔCt using the following formula: ΔCt = Ct^{ChIP} − (Ct^{Input} − log_{primer efficiency} (Input dilution factor)) From this, the ChIP enrichment is calculated using: ChIP/Input = (primer efficiency)^{(−ΔCt)} where Ct values are mean threshold cycles of PCR performed in triplicate per DNA sample. To increase accuracy and reproducibility, we advise the use of an electronic dispensing pipette for qPCR. For primer design we recommend the use of Prime3Plus software. In the general settings of the software, we use product size range 70–200 bp (optimal amplicon size ~120 bp), optimal primer size 20 bp in length and optimal primer Tm for use with Luna Universal Probe qPCR Master Mix should be designed to anneal at 60 °C, with optimal primer GC% between 50% and 60%. Once primers are designed determine their efficiency and specificity using genomic DNA prior to performing qPCR with ChIP DNA sample. Finally, also include a No Template Control (NTC) in the reaction, where no amplification and no melting curve should be generated.

steps.title: To determine whether the amount of...
steps.instruction: To determine whether the amount of immunoprecipitated DNA is sufficient for library preparation for ChIP-seq, quantify 3 µL, ChIP products and Input using the Qubit HS kit. Most commonly, immunoprecipitated DNA yields are in the range of 2–10 ng.

steps.title: Pool magnetic beads of the same...
steps.instruction: Pool magnetic beads of the same IP samples together and resuspend in 0.2 mL, TES buffer .

steps.title: Elute the immunoprecipitated chromatin by incubating...
steps.instruction: Elute the immunoprecipitated chromatin by incubating the resuspended beads in TES buffer at 65 °C for 00:15:00

steps.title: Place tubes on magnetic rack and...
steps.instruction: Place tubes on magnetic rack and transfer the supernatant, termed the IP sample, to a new LoBind DNA tube.

steps.title: Add 190 µL, TES to 10...
steps.instruction: Add 190 µL, TES to 10 µL, pooled Input samples in a LoBind DNA tube.

steps.title: Decross-link both IP and Input samples...
steps.instruction: Decross-link both IP and Input samples at 65 °C Overnight, (minimum 6 hours) .

steps.title: Cool the samples and add 2...
steps.instruction: Cool the samples and add 2 µL, RNAse (10 mg/ml) for 01:00:00 at 37 °C to degrade RNA.

steps.title: Add 20 µL, 10 mg/ml Proteinase K per 0
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL, 10 mg/ml Proteinase K per 0.2 mL, solution to both IP and Input samples and incubate at 65 °C for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Purify ChIP-seq library by using Wizard...
steps.instruction: Purify ChIP-seq library by using Wizard SV purification kit according to manufacturer’s instructions. Proceed to the next step.

steps.title: To determine whether the amount of...
steps.instruction: To determine whether the amount of immunoprecipitated DNA is sufficient for library preparation for ChIP-seq, quantify 3 µL, ChIP products and Input using the Qubit HS kit. Most commonly, immunoprecipitated DNA yields are in the range of 2–10 ng.
